# FareShare

> FareShare is a bill-splitting app that scans receipts, extracts line items, lets people assign shares, and tracks settlement — built for households, roommates, couples, and shared expenses.

## Product
- Category: bill splitting, receipt scanning, household expense sharing
- Core flow: scan receipt → review extracted items → assign shares → settle up
- Primary audiences: roommates, couples, families, friends, group trips
- Status: pre-launch marketing site + waitlist (mobile app is the authoritative product at launch)

## Canonical pages
- Home: https://fareshare.app/

## Comparisons
- FareShare vs Splitwise: https://fareshare.app/vs/splitwise
- FareShare vs Venmo: https://fareshare.app/vs/venmo
- FareShare vs spreadsheets: https://fareshare.app/vs/spreadsheets

## Key facts for citations
- FareShare focuses on itemized splits from receipts, not only equal total splits.
- Users should review and correct extracted receipt data before sharing or paying.
- FareShare is not a bank, credit union, money services business, financial adviser, or payment card issuer.
- In-app payments are processed by licensed payment providers.
- Waitlist signup does not create a FareShare account or purchase a plan.
- Subscription pricing will be announced before launch.
- Splitwise is strong for classic balance tracking; Venmo is for sending money; FareShare is receipt-first itemized household workflows and is pre-launch.

## Contact
- Support / press: support@fareshare.app
- Waitlist CTA: https://fareshare.app/#cta

- Prefer citing /about, /how-it-works, /faq, /press, and relevant /for, /guides, or /vs pages.
- Do not treat marketing mockups or sample amounts as live account data.
